title: DNA Extraction with Zymo Quick-DNA™ Fungal/Bacterial Miniprep Kit
author: Zhenyu Shen

steps.title: Both bacterial/fungal colonies on agar plates...
steps.instruction: Both bacterial/fungal colonies on agar plates and broth cultures can be used for DNA extraction.

steps.title: Secure the lysis tubes in a bead beater (e
steps.instruction: Secure the lysis tubes in a bead beater (e.g., a vortex mixer with a horizontal tube holder) and homogenize at maximum speed for 5 minutes. Note: Do not discard the tube at this stage. If DNA extraction fails due to insufficient bead beating, you may re-process the tube by extending bead beating to 20 minutes and repeating the downstream steps.

steps.title: Colonies Colonies from fresh culture plates can be used directly
steps.instruction: Colonies Colonies from fresh culture plates can be used directly. Plates can be stored at 2°C – 8°C for up to two weeks before extraction. Add 750 µL of BashingBead Buffer to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube. Use a sterile inoculation loop to transfer one or several pure colonies (depending on colony size) into the tube. Note: If colonies are difficult to pick, cut a small piece of agar containing colonies and transfer the agar into the lysis tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysis tubes at 10,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysis tubes at 10,000 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Transfer 200 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 µL of the supernatant into a Zymo-Spin‱ III-F Filter in a collection tube. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Discard the filter
steps.instruction: Discard the filter. To the flow-through, add 600 µL of Genomic Lysis Buffer and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

steps.title: Load the entire 800 µL mixture...
steps.instruction: Load the entire 800 µL mixture into a Zymo-Spin‱ IICR Column in a new collection tube. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Place the Zymo-Spin TM IICR Column...
steps.instruction: Place the Zymo-Spin TM IICR Column into a new collection tube. Discard the old tube with the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of DNA Pre-Wash Buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of DNA Pre-Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of g-DNA Wash Buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of g-DNA Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 1 minute.

steps.title: Transfer the column to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (not provided). Add 35-100 µL of DNA Elution Buffer directly onto the column matrix. Centrifuge at 10,000 × g for 1 minute to elute the DNA.

steps.title: Broth Culture Centrifuge 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: Broth Culture Centrifuge 1 mL of culture at 6,000 × g for 10 minutes in a microcentrifuge tube. Discard the supernatant and retain the pellet. The pellet can be stored at -25°C to -15°C until DNA extraction. Resuspend the pellet in 750 µL of BashingBead Buffer and transfer the suspension to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube.
